author: Tharusha Madushan
text:
Today, I called this number to buy a 17 Pro Max. Before coming, I asked all the details including the price and whether the phone was in stock. I asked twice if I could purchase it today if I came to the shop.

The person I spoke to clearly confirmed that the X/A version in orange colour was available. They also confirmed the price as 4.30 lakhs.

After confirming everything, I went to the shop around 11 AM, dropping some of my important work, because I trusted the name Rifkhan’s iDealz.

However, only after I arrived, they checked the stock and told me that the phone was not available. This is completely unacceptable. I did not go there to waste my time based on false information.

Is it fair for staff to mislead customers like this and make them come all the way for nothing? I was extremely disappointed and frustrated.

I do not recommend the Bambalapitiya branch at all. The staff there are not reliable. I don’t know about the other branches, but what happened here was a very unpleasant experience.

If the management is seeing this, please ensure that customers’ valuable time is not wasted by giving false information.

Completely disappointed.
owner_reply:
Response from the owner 2 months ago
Dear Tharusha,

We would like to clarify a few points to avoid any misunderstanding. During the phone conversation, there was no confirmation that you would be visiting the showroom immediately to purchase the device. The inquiry mainly related to pricing and trade-in value for your existing iPhone.At the time, the iPhone 17 Pro Max X/A SIM version in Orange was discussed, and when you visited iDeal Prime branch, that specific variant was unavailable. However, this was clearly communicated at the showroom, and our team offered you the eSIM version, which was available at a more budget-friendly price.Additionally, our staff informed you that the requested X/A SIM version could be arranged within approximately two days, with the understanding that the price may vary depending on availability.We strive to be transparent with our customers and do not intentionally provide misleading information. Nevertheless, we regret the inconvenience caused and appreciate your feedback, as it helps us improve communication and service clarity.

Team iDealz

I highly recommend idealz to anyone looking for reliable service, and a special thanks again to Ubaidhullah for his outstanding support. Definitely one of the best customer service experiences I’ve had. Keep up the great work!

author: Ananthraj Rajendran
text:
Do not buy Green Lion tempered glass.
20 days ago, I purchased the Green Lion 4-in-1 Defender Pack for my iPhone 17 Pro for 10,000/=. Initially, the price they said was 15,000/= but after I mentioned that their Liberty Plaza store was said it for 10,000/=, they agreed to match the price.

The main issue is the poor quality of the tempered glass included in the package. A very minor impact like a small key accidentally dropping on the screen caused the tempered glass to shatter. Idealz is charging premium prices for very low quality accessories, which is unfair and disappointing. I would not recommend green lion tempered glass based on my experience.

For those who wants to protect their new iphone I strongly recommend to go for ESR products which is available from Future world and Xclusive.

author: Nimasha Sewwandi
text:
My Honest Experience at Idealz Lanka - Marina Mall

I recently visited the Idealz Lanka store at Marina Mall to purchase a phone, and the sales representative Abdul Rahman was very approachable and helpful.

I purchased my Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra for LKR 271,240. However I experienced strong pressure to buy additional accessories. Initially Abdul Rahman quoted LKR 30,000 for a tempered glass screen protector, wireless earbuds, a phone back cover, and a charging adapter. When I declined he reduced the price to LKR 20,000 for just the tempered glass, earbuds, and charger.

While I appreciated Abdul Rahman's assistance, I would advise future customers to be cautious. There seems to be a push to upsell accessories at inflated prices, which can feel uncomfortable. If you're only looking to buy a phone, be firm in your decision and consider checking prices elsewhere before purchasing accessories from this store.

author: Buddhika Hettiarachchi
text:
I recently bought an **iPhone 16 Pro** from Idealz, and while I’m satisfied with the phone itself, my experience with their accessories pricing left me disappointed.

**The Positive:**
✔ **Great Phone Prices** – Their iPhone was cheaper than at other stores, which made the purchase worthwhile.

**The Big Negative:**
❌ **Ridiculous Markup on Accessories** – The salesperson initially quoted **LKR 33,000** for a **Green Lion 4-in-1 pack** (case, lens protector, and screen protector) + an apple charger .After some hesitation, they suddenly dropped the price to **LKR 24,000**, making it seem like a discount. But later, I found the **exact same pack at Liberty Plaza for just LKR 5,500!** That’s nearly **3 times cheaper**—a huge difference that made me feel tricked.

**My Advice:**
✅ **Buy your iPhone from Idealz** – They offer good deals on phones.
❌ **Avoid their accessories** – The inflated pricing and pushy sales tactics are a red flag. Always compare prices elsewhere before buying add-ons.

author: PickleRick
text:
I recently purchased a Samsung model here, but I’ve had a very concerning experience. Shortly after purchase, the screen began glitching and turning to static. When I examined the packaging to look for a solution, I noticed the original manufacturer seal had been cut with a blade and a second paper sticker had been placed over it to make it look unopened.
When I brought this to the store's attention, they denied any involvement in resealing units or selling non-original "new" phones. However, the physical evidence of the tampered seal suggests that these devices may be open-box or second-hand units being sold as brand new.
It seems this practice is particularly common with their budget-friendly models, though I would be cautious with their high-end inventory as well. I’m disappointed that a "new" purchase resulted in a defective screen and evidence of tampering. I strongly recommend that any future customers inspect the factory seals under a bright light before leaving the shop to ensure the box hasn't been double-stickered or it would best to seek another retailer altogether as this establishment is highly unreliable and its a gamble if your device is genuine

author: Palinda Attanayake
text:
We bought a MacBook Air with the M2 chip last year. After six months, the keyboard stopped working. They fixed it under warranty but charged us 10k for shipping, and it took about a month to repair (they said they replaced the motherboard). Then, after another 9 months, the laptop stopped working again—no response at all.

When I took it back to them, since it was no longer under warranty, they didn’t even check it and simply told me to fix it myself, claiming it wasn’t their responsibility anymore. Later, I took it to Abans for a checkup, and they confirmed that the motherboard was the issue again. Abans estimated the repair cost to be around 200k.

Buying a laptop for about 300k that stops working just over a year later is frustrating. If you’re planning to purchase something, it’s better to go with a reputed seller who provides good aftercare, with or without a warranty. No offense to Idealz—I bought it because it was cheaper, but if it keeps having issues like this, there’s no point in going for the cheaper option.

author: C D
text:
I was tricked, they are not genuine.

I purchased a Samsung A54 5G phone on 31st December 2023. Before even one year has passed, the phone got region locked. I was unable to make calls, send messages or even use data with any local sim.
Before buying the phone I inquired of the product's local compatibility? The sales person said not to worry and convinced me to buy it.
When I called the shop and told them the issue, the person who spoke seemed very unbothered and simply said "fix it from outside, it's because I have updated the phone".
I was outraged by their poor response (trying to blame me for a mandatory software update) and dishonesty. Because of this scam I was left in a very difficult situation without a phone. I am still to pay 2 more installments for this phone and I can't even use it. This is very unfair and I will be reporting this injustice to consumer affairs when I have the time.
